heroDesc: Move designs quickly from model to physical validation
bodyTitle: Rapid Prototype Service for appearance, structure and assembly validation
bodyDesc: Rapid prototype service focuses on validation speed and feedback. It is used for product appearance review, structural assembly, functional testing and pilot-build preparation. A prototype is not treated as the final production part; it helps teams identify geometry, material, assembly and appearance issues early.

panels.title: Validation Goal
panels.subtitle: Define First
panels.body: Appearance, dimension, assembly and function tests need different process choices.

panels.title: Process Match
panels.subtitle: Speed and Accuracy
panels.body: CNC, 3D printing, casting and sheet metal can be combined by goal.

panels.title: Iteration Support
panels.subtitle: Feedback
panels.body: Prototype results help adjust geometry, material and production route.

tableRows: Different goals require different materials and processes

steps.title: Define validation goal
steps.body: Explain whether samples are for appearance, assembly, function or customer review.

steps.title: Select prototype route
steps.body: Match CNC, 3D printing, casting or sheet metal by goal.

steps.title: Build prototypes
steps.body: Complete manufacturing, finishing and basic dimensional or visual check.

steps.title: Feedback and iterate
steps.body: Adjust geometry and manufacturing route based on prototype results.

asideCta.title: Submit Requirements
asideCta.body: Upload drawings and project goals so engineers can review the service route.

related.title: Related Service Combinations
related.desc: Complex projects often combine multiple services. Review by geometry, volume, material and delivery goals.

items.title: CNC Machining Service
items.body: Milling, turning, mill-turn and hole-pattern machining for metal and engineering plastic parts.

items.title: Molding Service
items.body: Injection molding, tool trials, low-volume molding and design optimization for plastic parts.

items.title: Custom Manufacturing Service
items.body: Coordinates CNC, sheet metal, finishing, purchased parts and assembly for complex projects.

faq.title: Rapid Prototype Service FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Rapid Prototype Service suitable for?
items.a: Rapid Prototype Service is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Rapid Prototype Service quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Rapid Prototype Service?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
